summary: TUI scraper fabricates healthy quota on parse failure
detail: _capture_usage() initializes remaining_pct=100 and appends the model even when the bar line fails the (\d+)%$ regex. Any scrollback line starting with 'Gemini', 'Claude', or 'GPT' is treated as a model block. Parse miss = fabricated 0%-used quota. Fix: fail-closed, return None/skip on parse miss.

summary: Authentication failures bypass and reset R15 suppression
detail: _fetch_gemini_cloudcode() collapses auth failures to None, classified as missing_token which never increments R15 counter. _agy_api_fetch_models() blanket-catches HTTP 401/403 as None. Both call _reset_auth_failures() on missing_token, erasing prior auth failures. Test locks in contradictory behavior.

summary: Fixed tmux session name causes destructive cross-process collisions
detail: Every process uses constant 'hermes-agy-quota' and unconditionally kill-sessions it at start. Two Hermes instances kill each other's in-flight scrape. Cleanup not finally-protected. Fix: PID/UUID-suffixed session name, try/finally.

summary: Global refresh flag + serial fetch: head-of-line blocking, possibly permanent
detail: One boolean refreshing flag for whole cache. Gemini path stacks 40+ seconds worst-case, blocking Claude/Codex/GLM/DeepSeek. Secret.Service.get_sync() has no timeout. Fix: per-provider refresh workers or deadline on keyring.

summary: Partial TUI viewport treated as authoritative, suppressing better fallbacks
detail: Scraper captures ~14 of ~33 lines. Any nonempty scrape returns immediately, skipping structured API and OAuth paths. Fix: demote scrape to best-effort supplement or merge with structured results.

summary: Non-Gemini pools contaminate the Gemini segment
detail: Scraper captures Gemini, Claude and GPT blocks, but _fetch_gemini_agy() groups all without provider filtering. Exhausted Claude/GPT pool renders red Ge segment while Gemini quota is healthy.

json_number_or_none() accepts NaN/±Infinity with no math.isfinite() check. NaN used-percentage clamps to 0% (falsely healthy). DeepSeek NaN balance renders as green $NaN. Fix: reject non-finite values.

summary: Gemini transport failures rendered as invalid API key
detail: fetch_gemini_quota() catches every models.list failure (timeout, DNS, HTTP 5xx) and returns key_valid=False. Network blip becomes false credential alarm Ge:KEY. Fix: distinguish transport failure from auth failure.

summary: Agy API response read without context manager or size cap
detail: Response body read without 1 MB cap or context manager. Drip-fed body can grow unboundedly while holding the sole refresh worker (compounds C4).

summary: Non-ISO reset_iso value in Agy path breaks field type contract
detail: Agy path stores '+3h'-style strings in reset_iso field. Every other producer/consumer treats it as ISO-8601. datetime.fromisoformat silently yields None. Store ISO form or use distinct key.

summary: Retained stale cache data never shown to user
detail: Cache retained on transient errors (test asserts this), but every renderer short-circuits to '?' when is_stale is set. 30s network blip degrades 2-minute-old reading to '?'. Either render retained value marked stale or stop retaining.

summary: Keyring fallback fragile and triggers desktop unlock prompts
detail: Substring-matches any label containing 'antigravity', blindly indexes ['token']['access_token'] with no shape validation, enumerates/unlocks entire keyring on every refresh. Fix: match exact attributes, validate shape, cache between refreshes.

summary: GLM fallback forwards credential to secondary host on auth failure
detail: GLM fallback forwards the same raw credential to open.bigmodel.cn on ANY primary failure including 401/403. Spec R3 mandates fallback only for host-availability failures. Skip fallback on auth rejection.
_fmt_reset_hours in __init__.py is dead code and collides confusingly with live _fmt_reset / _fmt_hours_until helpers.

summary: Hard-coded Google OAuth client secret lacks provenance comment
detail: gemini_cloudcode.py has hard-coded Google OAuth client secret. Acceptable for installed-app flows, but is a third party's credential reused by unaffiliated tool. Add provenance/ToS comment.
